title: nepeta camphorata var. camphorata
intro: Nepeta camphorata var. camphorata, also known as watermint or camphor catmint, is a plant species within the mint family that is characterized by its small blue or purple flowers and its distinctive camphor-like scent. The medicinal properties of Nepeta camphorata var. camphorata include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which make it useful for treating respiratory issues, wounds, and skin conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to relieve congestion, coughs, and colds, as well as its potential to reduce stress and anxiety. Herbal preparations of Nepeta camphorata var. camphorata can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
